_comment: Azure Resource Manager (ARM) Template — University API Management Instance
Defines your entire APIM configuration as code.
Deploy this file and Azure creates the APIM instance, products,
APIs, and policies automatically — no clicking through the portal.
- Reproducible: deploy identical config to dev, staging, production
- Version controlled: your gateway config lives in Git alongside your API code
- Reviewable: team can review gateway changes like any other code change
- Recoverable: if something breaks, you can redeploy the last known good config

metadata.description: Name of the APIM instance — must be globally unique in Azure
Azure region — use same region as your backend
URL of your FastAPI backend — Azure Container App or App Service URL

resources.comments2: Tells APIM where to forward requests after applying policies.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/university-api-backend')]

comments2: Named Values are APIM's secret store — like environment variables for policies.
Policies reference these by name, never hardcode secrets in policy XML.
In production: link this to Azure Key Vault instead of storing the value directly.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/jwt-secret-key')]

comments2: Products bundle APIs and define access tiers.
The student portal gets access to student, course, and library APIs.
Requires subscription (consumers must request access).
Auto-approved — no manual approval step needed for this product.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/student-portal')]

properties.description: Access for the student-facing portal application. Includes student profiles, course enrollment, and library borrowing.
properties.terms: This product is for use by the official university student portal application only.

properties.description: Access for faculty-facing applications. Read access to student data, full access to course management.
Full access to all APIs. IT administrators only.

comments2: Partner institutions get read-only access to public course data only.
approvalRequired: true means someone on your team must manually approve
each subscription request. Good for external/partner access.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/partner-readonly')]

properties.description: Limited read-only access to course catalog and program information for partner institutions.

comments2: This imports our OpenAPI spec into APIM.
In practice you'd use: az apim api import --specification-format OpenApi
The path '/v1' becomes the base URL suffix in APIM.
ServiceUrl points to our FastAPI backend.
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/university-api-v1')]

properties.description: Central API platform for university operations — Students, Courses, Faculty, Library

comments2: Applied to EVERY request through this APIM instance.
This is where you put cross-cutting concerns:
- CORS headers
- Request ID injection (correlation)
- Remove server headers (security)
- Global error format
resources.name: [concat(parameters('apimServiceName'), '/policy')]

properties.value:
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Inject a unique request ID for tracing across services -->
    <set-header name="X-Request-ID" exists-action="skip">
      <value>@(Guid.NewGuid().ToString())</value>
    </set-header>
    <!-- Pass the request ID to the backend -->
    <set-header name="X-Request-ID" exists-action="override">
      <value>@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("X-Request-ID", Guid.NewGuid().ToString()))</value>
    </set-header>
  </inbound>
  <backend>
    <forward-request />
  </backend>
  <outbound>
    <!-- Expose Request ID to consumers for support tickets -->
    <set-header name="X-Request-ID" exists-action="override">
      <value>@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("X-Request-ID"))</value>
    </set-header>
    <!-- Remove headers that reveal implementation details -->
    <delete-header name="X-Powered-By" />
    <delete-header name="Server" />
    <!-- Add API version header to every response -->
    <set-header name="X-API-Version" exists-action="override">
      <value>1.0.0</value>
    </set-header>
  </outbound>
  <on-error>
    <!-- Consistent error format for gateway-level errors -->
    <set-status code="@(context.Response.StatusCode)" />
    <set-header name="Content-Type" exists-action="override">
      <value>application/json</value>
    </set-header>
  </on-error>
</policies>

metadata.description: Your API gateway URL — this is what consumers call
Developer portal — where consumers read docs and request access
